content:
- Finished the ha-api-polls-hang-during-no-quorum task end-to-end.
- All required gates passed, task file marked completed/passes=true, task_switch ran, commit e2a6c94 was created, and git push to master succeeded.
- No AGENTS.md update felt necessary; quitting cleanly with a clean worktree.

- Read current progress log and scanned `.ralph/current_tasks.md`, `.ralph/current_tasks_done.md`, and task file paths.
- Resolved blocker state against done tasks. No active failing bug tasks are present in current_tasks. Active priorities are mostly normal, with the authoritative managed postgres config story carrying unblocked `high` priority work.
- Best next task is `story-authoritative-managed-postgres-config/01-task-introduce-a-typed-managed-postgres-conf-model-and-serializer.md` because it is the highest-priority unblocked failing task. Next step is to write that exact path into `.ralph/current_task.txt` and stop.

- Wrote the selected next-task path to `.ralph/current_task.txt`.
- Chosen task: `.ralph/tasks/story-authoritative-managed-postgres-config/01-task-introduce-a-typed-managed-postgres-conf-model-and-serializer.md`.
- Stopping immediately after task selection as instructed.

- Started new turn by reading Ralph progress and .ralph/current_task.txt.
- Active task is .ralph/tasks/story-authoritative-managed-postgres-config/01-task-introduce-a-typed-managed-postgres-conf-model-and-serializer.md.
- Next step is to inspect the task file and determine whether it needs initial planning, skeptical plan review, or direct execution.

- Read the active task, current managed-postgres implementation, startup/process/HA wiring, docs, and relevant tests.
- Key finding for the plan: authoritative managed config requires more than replacing the BTreeMap. Startup currently relies on ad hoc pg_ctl options and replica cloning still uses pg_basebackup -R, which writes Postgres-owned recovery settings.
- The execution draft will therefore include typed start intent, config_file cutover to pgtm.postgresql.conf, explicit standby.signal ownership, and removal of pg_basebackup -R so pgtuskmaster becomes the sole owner of startup GUCs.

- Wrote Draft 1 of the authoritative managed-postgres-config execution plan into the active task file.
- The task file now ends with TO BE VERIFIED, per the required workflow.
- Stopping here intentionally so the next pass can perform the skeptical plan review rather than drifting into execution.

- Started a new pass on the active authoritative managed postgres config task.
- Read prior progress and confirmed the active task path from .ralph/current_task.txt.
- Next I am checking the task phase marker so I can either do the skeptical review or continue the workflow exactly.

- Verified the task file is in TO BE VERIFIED and started a skeptical review instead of implementation.
- Reviewed runtime startup, HA lowering/dispatch, postgres_managed, process worker command building, and the live startup/logging tests.
- Concrete correction found: the HA problem is not apply-ordering but missing leader context on PostgresEffect::Start / HaAction::StartPostgres, because FollowLeader currently becomes a skipped action.
- Also confirmed the startup e2e test still expects SHOW config_file = PGDATA/postgresql.conf and the logging ingest real test appends directly to postgresql.conf, so the execution plan must call these out explicitly before moving to NOW EXECUTE.

- Completed the skeptical review pass and changed the task file plan in concrete ways before advancing it.
- Main plan corrections: thread typed start intent from runtime planning into the start job, and fix HA by carrying leader context on StartPostgres instead of treating apply ordering as the main issue.
- Advanced the task marker from TO BE VERIFIED to NOW EXECUTE.
- Starting implementation now with the typed managed-config domain and parser/schema support, then start-flow refactors, tests, docs, and the required make gates.

- Implemented the new authoritative managed postgres config domain and rewired config parsing, managed file materialization, runtime startup, HA dispatch, and process start command building around it.
- cargo check now passes and make check passes; along the way I fixed widespread constructor fallout for the new postgres.extra_gucs field, updated the startup e2e config_file expectation, and moved the logging ingest real test onto the managed config path.
- Next is the heavier validation: make test, then make test-long and make lint, fixing any behavioral regressions they reveal before marking task completion and ticking the task file boxes.

- Reloaded progress and confirmed the active task is still the authoritative managed postgres config story in NOW EXECUTE state.
- make check and make test were already green, but make test-long failed in the no-quorum fencing integrity scenario: after quorum loss all three nodes ended up refusing SQL connections during the final integrity check.
- I inspected the failing test and HA dispatch start-intent helper; current suspicion is a behavioral regression in long-running HA/no-quorum handling, so next I am reproducing only that test with focused logs and then patching the responsible state/startup path.

- New inspection turn focused on HA decision/action path for preserving leader context into StartPostgres without re-deriving from DCS cache.
- Read prior progress and confirmed earlier suspicion already noted: leader context is computed upstream but HaAction::StartPostgres currently carries no payload, so follow-leader context likely gets lost before HA process dispatch.
- Next I am reading ha/actions, ha/worker, ha/events, and runtime/node to identify the exact minimal type/file/test changes and any knock-on effects, without editing files.

- Confirmed the concrete re-derivation point: src/ha/process_dispatch.rs builds ManagedPostgresStartIntent for HaAction::StartPostgres by calling start_intent_from_dcs(), which inspects the latest DCS cache again.
- runtime/node already has the more appropriate typed model: startup selection resolves a specific leader member and turns it into ManagedPostgresStartIntent::Replica upstream, then StartPostgres just consumes that typed intent.
- Next I am checking the HA decision/decide path to see whether minimal threading should start at HaDecision/HaEffectPlan/PostgresEffect or only at HaAction::StartPostgres, and which existing tests/assertions depend on the old no-payload StartPostgres shape.

- Important refinement: action-only change is insufficient. Leader identity is already lost when decide.rs emits HaDecision::WaitForPostgres { start_requested } after replica/recovery paths, so preserving context through StartPostgres requires widening WaitForPostgres (or equivalent state) upstream.
- That implies a minimal end-to-end thread likely spans HaDecision -> HaEffectPlan/PostgresEffect -> HaAction::StartPostgres -> process_dispatch, plus API/CLI mapping for the widened WaitForPostgres response shape.
- Now collecting exact line references and the concrete tests that would need updates or additions so the final answer can be precise without editing anything.

- The worktree changed during inspection; the earlier decide.rs compile error disappeared on reread, so I treated the tree as actively moving and anchored the analysis to the latest on-disk code.
- Key conclusion solidified: the real remaining behavioral hole is in src/ha/process_dispatch.rs, where start_intent_from_dcs still falls back to leader_member_from_cache when WaitForPostgres.leader_member_id is None. That still re-derives start role/leader from live DCS and can override the preserved decision context.
- Final response will call out both the hidden coupling on ctx.state being updated before apply_effect_plan and the tests that should pin primary-with-foreign-leader and replica-preserved-leader behavior.

- Reproduced the long no-quorum failure with PGTM_TEST_KEEP_NAMESPACE=1 and found the real regression: replica nodes were ending up with primary-style managed config (`hot_standby = off`, missing standby.signal) after HA-driven restarts, then promoting/crashing after the fenced primary disappeared.
- Began patching HA so WaitForPostgres carries replica leader context forward and StartPostgres dispatch rebuilds replica intent from that preserved leader instead of guessing from current degraded DCS state.
- Also hit recurring cargo linker flakiness after edits/clean rebuilds, so I am switching to clean sequential non-incremental builds for verification to avoid false negatives from target-dir corruption.

- Resumed execution on the authoritative managed postgres config task.
- Current blocker is a compile failure in integration builds because process_dispatch now imports parse_pg_conninfo, but conninfo parsing is still hidden behind #[cfg(test)].
- Next I am promoting the parser/error types to normal crate code, then rerunning focused tests and the failing long HA scenario.

- Promoted conninfo parser/error code out of #[cfg(test)] so HA dispatch can parse preserved primary_conninfo in normal builds.
- Running focused HA/lib tests now to make sure the replica-preservation and WaitingDcsTrusted changes still hold after exposing the parser.
- After these are green I will rerun the failing no-quorum long integration repro with clean non-incremental settings.

- Integration compile caught one more test-only gate in conninfo: the private Cursor helper struct was still behind #[cfg(test)].
- Removed that too; the runtime parser stack should now compile fully in normal builds.
- Retrying the no-quorum long integration repro now.

- The previously failing no-quorum long integration repro is now green with the preserved-replica/WaitForPostgres leader-context changes.
- That confirms the main regression was in restart role preservation, not a deeper DCS-trust promotion bug for this scenario.
- Starting the required full verification gates now: make check, make test, make test-long, make lint, all with clean non-incremental settings to reduce linker flakiness.

- Required make test run found one remaining regression: ha::decide::tests::actions_are_reissued_while_conditions_persist.
- This is likely fallout from widening WaitForPostgres with leader context and changing WaitingDcsTrusted behavior.
- Reproducing that single test now and reading the assertion before patching the decision/test mismatch.

- The make test failure was a stale unit assumption, not a runtime bug: WaitingDcsTrusted replicas with no leader now intentionally wait instead of campaigning.
- Updated the reissue test to cover the still-valid case where local Postgres is primary and leadership acquisition should be retried while conditions persist.
- Rerunning the targeted unit and then the full make test gate.

- make check and make test are now fully green.
- The stale WaitingDcsTrusted reissue test has been updated to assert lease reacquisition only when the local node is already primary.
- Running make test-long now; if that passes, only make lint and task finalization remain.

- make test-long is midway through and healthy: failover, strict failsafe, the previously failing integrity-preservation scenario, and both stress scenarios are already green.
- Once the remaining four long HA scenarios finish, only make lint plus task bookkeeping/commit/push remain.
- No new behavioral failures have surfaced since the replica-preservation fix.

- make test-long finished fully green: all 9 ultra-long HA scenarios passed.
- make lint is now the last hard gate; clippy is running against the full target set.
- If lint passes cleanly, I will update the task file passes flag, run task_switch, commit all changes including .ralph, and push.

- make lint failed on a single clippy violation in runtime/node.rs: an unnecessary reference comparison (clippy::op_ref).
- This is a straightforward code cleanup, not a design issue.
- Patching that line now and then rerunning the full lint gate.

- All required gates are green now: make check, make test, make test-long, and make lint.
- Entering final bookkeeping: update the active task file checkboxes and passes flag, then run task_switch, commit all changes including .ralph metadata, and push.
- After that I will stop immediately as requested.
